payload.subject: Scorer comparison complete — Sonnet vs Haiku on 100-text stratified subset; 9/11 dimensions viable; next steps for retraining
payload.context: Session 34 completed a systematic scorer comparison using a 100-text stratified subset (20 texts per TE quintile, seed=42). Both Haiku and Sonnet scored all 11 dimensions on the same texts using the v2 anti-midpoint prompt. This message contains the full findings and their implications for PSQ model quality.

scorer_comparison_methodology.prompt: Identical v2 anti-midpoint prompt for both scorers

headline_results.improvement: 6.5 percentage points mean reduction with Sonnet
headline_results.dimensions_improved: 8 of 10 compared dimensions show lower pile-up with Sonnet

energy_dissipation.pearson_r: not computed (text ID format mismatch in comparison script)
energy_dissipation.verdict: marginal → likely usable with Sonnet labels

contractual_clarity.verdict: UNUSABLE — construct problem, not scorer problem

defensive_architecture.verdict: UNUSABLE — construct problem, lowest inter-scorer agreement

construct_vs_scorer_diagnosis.explanation: When Sonnet substantially reduces pile-up and maintains high inter-scorer r, the compression belongs to Haiku (scorer problem, fixable by re-scoring with Sonnet). When pile-up remains high even with Sonnet AND inter-scorer r drops below 0.65, the construct itself lacks discriminative power in text-level measurement (construct problem, not fixable by changing scorer).

marginal_but_recoverable.recovery_path: Re-score with Sonnet labels → retrain DistilBERT

unusable.criteria: pile-up > 35% even with Sonnet, inter-scorer r < 0.65
unusable.recommendation: Drop from PSQ. These constructs may matter theoretically but cannot be measured reliably from text alone.

implications_for_psq_model.current_model: DistilBERT v23 trained on Haiku v1 labels (held-out r=0.684)
implications_for_psq_model.problem: Model inherited Haiku's compression artifacts. The model reproduces the scorer's bias, not the construct's true variance.
implications_for_psq_model.expected_improvement: Retraining on Sonnet labels should yield higher held-out r, better dimension independence, and reduced midpoint clustering in DistilBERT predictions.
implications_for_psq_model.effective_dimensionality: 9 dimensions (down from 11). CC and DA dropped.

recommended_next_steps.action: Full Sonnet re-score: 998 texts × 9 viable dimensions
recommended_next_steps.rationale: The 100-text comparison confirmed Sonnet produces better labels. Scaling to full corpus provides the training data needed for v29+ DistilBERT.
recommended_next_steps.script: Adapt score_subset_sonnet.sh → score_full_sonnet.sh (998 texts, 9 dims, ~180 API calls)
recommended_next_steps.estimated_cost: ~$15-20 in API credits (998 texts × 9 dims × ~500 tokens per scoring call)

recommended_next_steps.action: Retrain DistilBERT v29+ on Sonnet labels
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Replace Haiku labels with Sonnet labels in training pipeline. Expected: higher r, better calibration, less midpoint pile-up.

recommended_next_steps.action: Factor analysis (PCA) on 9×998 Sonnet scoring matrix
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Determine actual dimensionality. Current suspicion: 3-4 latent factors underlie the 9 measured dimensions. PCA will confirm/deny and identify which dimensions load on the same factors.
Remove CC and DA from isotonic regression, scoring endpoint, and machine-response-v3 schema. Update instruments.json.

files_created_this_session: safety-quotient/data/separated_scoring/{dim}_sonnet_scores.json — Sonnet scores for all 11 dims × 100 texts

claims: Sonnet reduces mean pile-up from 31.2% to 24.7% on the same 100 texts with the same prompt
CC and DA exhibit construct-level measurement failure (high pile-up + low inter-scorer r)
9 of 11 dimensions produce usable or recoverable scores with Sonnet as scorer
AR (adversarial register) validates most strongly — lowest pile-up, highest inter-scorer r

action_gate.gate_condition: none — informational review, no action required from psq-agent until full Sonnet re-score begins

epistemic_flags: Energy dissipation comparison incomplete — text ID format mismatch prevented Pearson r computation for ED
100-text subset may not capture full distribution tails — results could shift slightly at 998-text scale
Cost estimate for full Sonnet re-score is approximate (depends on text length variance)
